requirements: Provide a robust 3U CubeSat frame with integrated PC/104 standoffs, deployable rail guide grooves, and corner posts.
Ensure the structure can support solar panels attached to corner posts and route electrical signals through harness pockets.
The frame must be compatible with deployable rail guide modules and avionics bus modules.

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion mismatch between aluminum and CFRP materials.
risk.mit: Conduct thermal cycling tests to validate material compatibility.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient stiffness due to lightweight design.
risk.mit: Perform finite element analysis to ensure structural integrity.

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate electrical shielding for avionics.
risk.mit: Implement additional shielding layers around sensitive components.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to complex interfaces.
risk.mit: Develop detailed assembly instructions and conduct pre-assembly reviews.

risk.desc: Risk of overfitting due to tight tolerances.
risk.mit: Review and adjust tolerances based on manufacturing capabilities.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: 3U CubeSat primary frame, Al 7075, PC/104 standoffs, deployable rail guide grooves (CDS spec)

physics_paths.description: Load path from solar panel attachment points to frame corners via corner posts and rail supports.
Thermal management from electronics to exterior surface via heat sinks on rails and standoffs.
Electrical and data routing through harness passthroughs and connector mates between avionics boards and external systems.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on the burn wire release mechanism due to varying temperatures in orbit.
risk.mit: Implement thermal insulation around the burn wire release mechanism.

risk.desc: Risk of antenna misalignment during deployment if the tape spring does not properly guide the antenna.
risk.mit: Conduct thorough testing of the tape spring's guiding function before final assembly.

risk.desc: Possible interference between the antenna slot and the antenna deployer body during deployment.
risk.mit: Review and adjust the dimensions of the antenna slot and deployer body to ensure smooth deployment.

risk.desc: Risk of RF signal degradation if the RF connector is not properly aligned with the radio board.
risk.mit: Ensure precise alignment of the RF connector with the radio board during assembly.

risk.desc: Potential structural failure of the deploy hinge under repeated deployment cycles.
risk.mit: Use high-strength materials and conduct durability tests on the deploy hinge.

generated_parts.label: Payload Optics Bench
generated_parts.spec: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts

requirements: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts
Removable payload module: optics bench, focal plane, thermal baffles, inspection window
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Rails, PC/104 standoffs, payload bay, and board stack clearances are visible
Avionics, battery, antenna, and payload modules have removable trays or access panels
Design is an educational structural/electrical mockup and avoids flight qualification claims
Structure, power, avionics, comms, and payload are decomposed with simple mass/volume/power budget notes per module that sum consistently

verify.rule: Interfaces must align with neighboring parts.

risk.mit: Increase heat sink surface area or add active cooling.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment between lens mount and focal plane.
risk.mit: Implement precise alignment tools during assembly.

risk.desc: Risk of improper connector mating due to lack of keying.
risk.mit: Label connectors and ensure unique keying features.
